verses.text: They seek a sign; He says they read the sky but not 'the signs of the times'; only the sign of Jonas. Blind to the obvious. They demand proof while ignoring the One before them.

They seek a sign; He says they read the sky but not 'the signs of the times'; only the sign of Jonas. Blind to the obvious. They demand proof while ignoring the One before them.
He warns of 'the leaven of the Pharisees'; the disciples think He means literal bread. Slow to grasp. They miss the spiritual meaning behind His words.
He warns of 'the leaven of the Pharisees'; the disciples think He means literal bread. Slow to grasp. They miss the spiritual meaning behind His words.
He warns of 'the leaven of the Pharisees'; the disciples think He means literal bread. Slow to grasp. They miss the spiritual meaning behind His words.
Recalling the loaves He multiplied, He helps them see He means the false doctrine of the Pharisees. Beware teaching. A little error spreads like leaven through the whole.

'Whom say ye that I am?' Peter answers, 'Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God.' The great confession. Here is the rock-truth on which all faith stands.
'Whom say ye that I am?' Peter answers, 'Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God.' The great confession. Here is the rock-truth on which all faith stands.
'Whom say ye that I am?' Peter answers, 'Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God.' The great confession. Here is the rock-truth on which all faith stands.
'Whom say ye that I am?' Peter answers, 'Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God.' The great confession. Here is the rock-truth on which all faith stands.
Blessed art thou; flesh and blood revealed it not, but My Father; 'upon this rock I will build my church.' Divine revelation. Saving faith is God's gift, and the church is built on Christ confessed.
Blessed art thou; flesh and blood revealed it not, but My Father; 'upon this rock I will build my church.' Divine revelation. Saving faith is God's gift, and the church is built on Christ confessed.
He gives 'the keys of the kingdom,' with power to bind and loose. Stewardship. The apostles open the kingdom by preaching the gospel.
He gives 'the keys of the kingdom,' with power to bind and loose. Stewardship. The apostles open the kingdom by preaching the gospel.
He foretells His suffering and death; Peter rebukes Him, and is told, 'Get thee behind me, Satan.' Mistaken love. Even good intentions oppose God when they shun the cross.
He foretells His suffering and death; Peter rebukes Him, and is told, 'Get thee behind me, Satan.' Mistaken love. Even good intentions oppose God when they shun the cross.
He foretells His suffering and death; Peter rebukes Him, and is told, 'Get thee behind me, Satan.' Mistaken love. Even good intentions oppose God when they shun the cross.

'If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross'; what profits gaining the world but losing the soul? The cost. Following Christ means self-denial and the soul's eternal gain.

'If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross'; what profits gaining the world but losing the soul? The cost. Following Christ means self-denial and the soul's eternal gain.

'If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross'; what profits gaining the world but losing the soul? The cost. Following Christ means self-denial and the soul's eternal gain.
The Son of man shall come in glory to reward every man; some shall see His kingdom come. Sure recompense. He who calls to the cross also promises the crown.
